# VER-QUIC-RFC9000-0012 - QUIC RFC 9000 ACK_ECN Frame Codec Verification

## Scope

This verification covers the supported ACK and ACK_ECN frame-codec floor: accepting ACK frame types 0x02 and 0x03, requiring all three ECN count fields for type 0x03, rejecting unsupported ACK type values and serializer type/count mismatches, encoding each ECN count as a QUIC variable-length integer, and preserving parsed cumulative count values.

## Requirements Verified

- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3-0001
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3-0003
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3-0009
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3P2-0001
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3P2-0002
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3P2-0003
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3P2-0004
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3P2-0005
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3P2-0006
- REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S19P3P2-0007

## Verification Method

Requirement-home execution, deterministic frame-codec fuzz coverage, BenchmarkDotNet Dry validation, and SpecTrace render/check for the bounded ACK_ECN frame-codec slice.

## Preconditions

- The frame codec receives a complete contiguous ACK frame payload.
- The caller uses `QuicAckFrame.FrameType` to distinguish type 0x02 from type 0x03.
- The caller uses `QuicEcnCounts` only for the three cumulative ACK_ECN counters and not as proof of endpoint ECN deployment.

## Procedure or Approach

- Run the focused RFC 9000 ACK and ACK_ECN requirement-home tests for S19P3-0001, S19P3-0003, S19P3-0009, and S19P3P2 requirements.
- Confirm deterministic ACK frame fuzz coverage remains linked for representative ACK/ACK_ECN round-trips and truncation rejection.
- Run `QuicFrameCodecBenchmarks` with the Dry job.
- Render and check the touched SpecTrace artifacts.
- Confirm that no receiver IP ECN access, sender validation, congestion response, runtime ACK scheduling, public API, or interop ECN claim is introduced.

## Expected Result

Focused tests prove positive and negative ACK_ECN frame-codec behavior, deterministic fuzz coverage exercises representative ACK/ACK_ECN shapes and truncation rejection, and the BenchmarkDotNet Dry suite executes representative parse and format hot-path cases without treating Dry output as a stable performance baseline.

## Status

Passed locally on 2026-04-25. Focused ACK/ACK_ECN requirement-home tests passed 14/14, and the updated QuicFrameCodecBenchmarks Dry suite executed all 10 frame-codec cases including ACK_ECN parse and format. Dry benchmark results are harness evidence only and are not treated as stable performance baselines.

## Related Artifacts

- SPEC-QUIC-RFC9000
- ARC-QUIC-RFC9000-0012
- WI-QUIC-RFC9000-0012

## Fuzz And Benchmark Scope

The deterministic requirement-home fuzz proof covers representative ACK and ACK_ECN round-trips plus truncation rejection through the frame-codec entry point. The permanent BenchmarkDotNet suite covers ACK_ECN parsing and formatting alongside the existing frame-codec cases; Dry validation is harness evidence only and is not treated as a stable performance baseline.
